title: ANDROGRAPHIS
description: Andrographis paniculata, also known as 'King of Bitters,' is a medicinal plant traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine. Its primary active compound, andrographolide, demonstrates antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and immune-modulating properties by interfering with viral replication pathways and regulating inflammatory cytokines. It is primarily used for respiratory infections, immune support, and inflammatory conditions.

research.title: Andrographolide as a potent and promising antiviral agent
research.summary: Andrographolide demonstrates broad antiviral activity with low cytotoxicity, preventing viral spread and transmission by interfering with cell signaling pathways, making it cost-effective and promising for fighting viral infections.

research.title: Mechanistic Evidence of Andrographis paniculata in the Treatment of Acute, Uncomplicated Respiratory Tract Infections
research.summary: Systematic review demonstrates Andrographis has promising clinical effects in treating uncomplicated acute upper respiratory tract infections, with evidence of antimicrobial activity and potential to reduce inappropriate antibiotic use.

research.title: An Andrographis paniculata extract prevents house dust mite-induced airway inflammation, remodeling, and hyperreactivity
research.summary: Standardized Andrographis extract demonstrated protective anti-inflammatory effects in a mouse asthma model by regulating Th1/Th2 gene expression and preventing airway inflammation and remodeling.

research.title: Herbal Medicines for the Treatment of Active Ulcerative Colitis: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
research.summary: Systematic review evaluating randomized controlled trials of herbal medicines including Andrographis for treating active ulcerative colitis, examining clinical response and remission outcomes.

research.title: Systematic review of complementary and alternative medicine treatments in inflammatory bowel diseases
research.summary: Comprehensive review of CAM treatments including Andrographis for inflammatory bowel diseases, evaluating evidence for efficacy and safety in Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.

research.title: The role of dietary supplements in inflammatory bowel disease: a systematic review
research.summary: Systematic review assessing dietary supplements including Andrographis paniculata for IBD management, evaluating evidence for food supplementation effectiveness.

sentiment.summary: Users consistently report Andrographis as highly effective for shortening illness duration and preventing colds/flu, with many describing it as a 'game changer' for immune support. While some users note it should be used acutely rather than long-term, the overwhelming majority report positive experiences with minimal side effects when used appropriately.

effects.label: Acute Illness:
effects.description: Most effective when taken at first sign of cold/flu symptoms, with users reporting 3-5x daily dosing during active infections for best results

effects.label: Preventive Use:
effects.description: Users report success using it preventively during cold/flu season, though most recommend cycling rather than continuous long-term use

effects.label: Combination Therapy:
effects.description: Often stacked with echinacea, olive leaf extract, vitamin C, and zinc for enhanced immune support during illness

effects.label: Research Validation:
effects.description: Multiple systematic reviews and clinical studies support its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly for respiratory infections

dosage.label: Acute Illness:
dosage.description: 500mg taken 3-5 times daily during active cold/flu symptoms for maximum effectiveness

dosage.label: Preventive:
dosage.description: 500mg once or twice daily during high-risk periods or cold/flu season

dosage.label: Standardization:
dosage.description: Most effective products are standardized to andrographolide content, with research using extracts containing 10-50mg andrographolides

dosage.label: Cycling Recommended:
dosage.description: Users and practitioners suggest not using continuously long-term, instead cycling on/off or using only when needed

sideEffects.label: Blood Pressure Effects:
sideEffects.description: Can lower blood pressure and heart rate, potentially causing lethargy if used excessively or in combination with other blood pressure-lowering supplements

sideEffects.label: Acne/Hormonal Effects:
sideEffects.description: Some users report forehead acne when taking Andrographis, possibly due to effects on testosterone or hormonal pathways

sideEffects.label: Bleeding Risk:
sideEffects.description: Research notes potential to slow blood clotting, important consideration before surgery or when combined with anticoagulants

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Most users report minimal side effects when used at recommended doses, with low cytotoxicity noted in research

availability.label: Brand Recommendations:
availability.description: Users recommend reputable brands with third-party testing, though availability can vary

availability.label: Formulations:
availability.description: Available as capsules (most common), extracts, and powders, with standardized extracts being preferred for consistent dosing

availability.label: Price Point:
availability.description: Generally affordable, with budget brands like quality brands offering it in clearance sales for $2-3 per bottle, while premium brands like Gaia Herbs are more expensive but highly trusted
